subject: Geology
title: The Carboniferous Period
description: A fast breakdown of The Carboniferous Period for builders.

hook.text: 300 million years ago, vast swamp forests covered the land. When they died and were buried, they became coal.

buildup.text: The Carboniferous period had warm, humid climates and huge forests. Dead plant matter piled up in swamps.

discovery.text: Insects grew enormous. Dragonflies had 70-cm wingspans. High oxygen levels let arthropods reach giant sizes.

twist.text: The coal we burn today is stored Carboniferous sunlight. We are releasing energy captured 300 million years ago.

climax.text: The Carboniferous shaped our energy economy. Those ancient swamps fueled the Industrial Revolution centuries later.

punchline.text: Ancient swamps power the modern world.

quiz.question: Why did insects grow so large in the Carboniferous?
options: High oxygen levels allowed larger arthropod body sizes
